data.title: Guardant Health files lawsuit against Natera for stealing trade secrets
data.description:
• Cancer test maker Guardant Health (NASDAQ:GH) has filed a lawsuit in a California district court this week alleging that its rival Natera (NASDAQ:NTRA) stole its trade secrets by recruiting its scientists, whom it said had illegally transferred company data before their resignations.
  • In the lawsuit filed at the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California on Thursday, the company argued that “unable to compete fairly, Natera recently turned its focus on not only poaching Guardant’s employees but stealing Guardant’s trade secrets.”
  • GH alleged that two of its employees, Alan Selewa and Catalin Barbacioru, resigned this year to join Natera (NASDAQ:NTRA) but not before illegally transferring more than 6,500 key trade secrets from its IT system.
  • The company seeks a court order forcing a return of all transferred trade secret information and unspecified monetary damages.

data.title: Twin Peaks is in the spotlight as Hooters heads toward a bankruptcy filing
data.description:
Hooters of America is reportedly working with creditors on a plan to restructure the business through bankruptcy court. Sources told Bloomberg that they expect the bankruptcy process to formally begin in the next few months.

The restaurant company operates the Hooters chain as a sports bar concept that features skimpy server uniforms. The chain was founded in Clearwater, Florida, by six businessmen with no prior restaurant experience. The concept combined casual dining, sports, and attractive waitresses known as "Hooters Girls". Initially struggling, the restaurant gained popularity after NFL players visited during the 1984 Super Bowl. The chain expanded throughout the U.S. and internationally in the 1980s and 1990s before expansion slowed down.

The Hooters development could be relevant for Twin Hospitality Group (NASDAQ:TWNP), which was recently spun off from FAT Brands (FAT). The company runs the Twin Peaks chain, which competes directly for sales against Hooters and other restaurant operators such as Darden Restaurants (DRI), Brinker International (EAT), Buffalo Wild Wings, Tilted Kilt, and Wingstop (WING).

data.title: Atmus projects $1.67B-$1.735B in 2025 revenue with 19%-20% EBITDA margin target
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Atmus Filtration Technologies (NYSE:ATMU) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Steph Disher highlighted the company's strong financial performance in Q4 2024, with sales reaching $407 million, supported by higher volumes and pricing actions. The company also completed $20 million in share repurchases during the year and has $130 million authorized for 2025.
  • The company emphasized progress on its 4-pillar growth strategy, including a new business win with a major European OEM for fuel filtration and crankcase ventilation, and the launch of the NanoNet N3 media technology to expand fuel filtration capabilities.
  • Operational separation from Cummins is 95% complete, with full independence expected in 2025. Adjusted EBITDA margin expanded by 410 basis points since 2022, supported by supply chain transformation and cost reduction efforts.
  • CFO Jack Kienzler reported adjusted EBITDA of $78 million in Q4, representing 19.1% of sales, compared to 17.9% in the previous year. Adjusted EPS for the quarter was $0.58, and adjusted free cash flow was $28 million.

Outlook

  • Atmus anticipates 2025 revenue in the range of $1.67 billion to $1.735 billion, reflecting a flat to 4% growth compared to 2024. Adjusted EBITDA margin is projected between 19% and 20%.
  • Adjusted EPS for 2025 is expected to range between $2.35 and $2.60.
  • The company expects aftermarket revenue to be flat to up 3%, supported by a 2% market outperformance and a 1% pricing increase, offset by a 2% headwind from a strong U.S. dollar.
  • First-fit markets are expected to face challenging conditions, with U.S. heavy-duty production flat to down 10%, and medium-duty production down 5% to 15%. India and China markets are anticipated to remain weak.

Financial Results

  • Sales in Q4 2024 increased by 1.8% year-over-year to $407 million, driven by a 2% increase in volumes and a 1% pricing benefit, partially offset by a 1% foreign exchange headwind.
  • Gross margin reached $107 million, supported by lower manufacturing costs, though higher logistics and material costs partially offset these benefits.
  • For the full year 2024, sales rose 2.5% to $1.67 billion, with adjusted EBITDA reaching $330 million, equating to a 19.7% margin. Adjusted EPS for the year was $2.03, and adjusted free cash flow totaled $115 million.
  • One-time costs related to separation from Cummins amounted to $25 million for the year, with an additional $5 million to $10 million expected in 2025.

Q&A

  • Joe O'Dea, Wells Fargo: Questioned the sustainability of EBITDA margin improvement and quarterly cadence for 2025. CFO Jack Kienzler explained that foreign exchange headwinds, rising input costs, and a lag in pricing adjustments could impact early 2025 margins, with sequential improvements expected later in the year.
  • Rob Mason, Baird: Asked about seasonality and first-half revenue weighting. CEO Steph Disher noted that aftermarket recovery and first-fit market rebounds are more likely in the second half of 2025, shifting traditional seasonality patterns.
  • Bobby Brooks, Northland Capital Markets: Inquired about mitigating tariff impacts on manufacturing in China and Mexico. Disher outlined pricing actions and supply chain flexibility as key strategies.
  • Jerry Revich, Goldman Sachs: Asked about incremental margin expansion opportunities. Disher stated that the focus is on sustaining the 19%-20% EBITDA margin range while driving growth through supply chain transformation and value engineering.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts showed a neutral to slightly positive tone, focusing on margin sustainability, growth initiatives, and market recovery timelines. Questions reflected cautious optimism about the company's ability to navigate challenging market conditions.
  • Management displayed confidence during prepared remarks, emphasizing strategic wins and financial discipline. However, during exchanges with analysts, the tone shifted slightly defensive when addressing market headwinds and tariff-related uncertainties.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management maintained a consistent level of confidence but provided more detailed explanations regarding the impacts of macroeconomic pressures.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2025 revenue ($1.67 billion to $1.735 billion) showed an increase over the previous quarter's outlook for 2024 ($1.65 billion to $1.675 billion).
  • Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance remained consistent at 19%-20%, compared to the prior quarter's raised range of 19.25%-19.75% for 2024.
  • Analysts' focus shifted from transition-related costs in Q3 to broader market recovery timelines and tariff impacts in Q4.
  • Management tone remained confident but included more granular insights into supply chain and pricing strategies.

Risks and Concerns

  • Weak market conditions in China and India are expected to persist into 2025, with limited visibility into recovery timelines.
  • Foreign exchange headwinds and rising input costs, particularly steel, were highlighted as key challenges.
  • Tariff uncertainties, particularly related to U.S.-Mexico trade, could impact manufacturing costs and supply chain dynamics.
  • Restructuring actions in China and the U.S. signal ongoing adjustments to navigate soft market conditions.

Final Takeaway

Atmus Filtration Technologies delivered solid Q4 2024 results and outlined a cautious yet optimistic outlook for 2025, projecting revenue growth and sustained strong margins despite challenging market conditions. Key strategic initiatives, including investments in technology, supply chain transformation, and targeted market expansion, are expected to support resilience and growth. However, analysts raised concerns about macroeconomic headwinds, tariff uncertainties, and the timing of market recoveries, underscoring the importance of execution in the coming year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Apple Intelligence plans to add more languages and make its debut in EU
data.description:
Apple Intelligence (NASDAQ:AAPL), the tech giant's personalized AI system, is being introduced in several new languages and is finally coming to European Union nations, beginning in April, the company announced today.

The new languages include French, German, Italian, Portuguese, Spanish, Korean and Chinese. It will also be released in localized English versions for Singapore and India.

The new languages will coincide with the release of a series of operating system updates, including iOS 18.4, iPadOS 18.4 and macOS Sequoia 15.4. These will be released today for developers.

The updates will also bring Apple Intelligence to iPhone and iPad users in European Union nations for the first time. Apple Intelligence features will expand to the Vision Pro as well.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Nvidia earnings, Amazon product event, PayPal Investor Day and Buffett's letter
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

Saturday - February 22

  • Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A) (BRK.B) CEO Warren Buffett will release his annual shareholder letter alongside the company's earnings report.

Monday - February 24

  • Volatility watch - Super Micro Computer (SMCI) and D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) have a high level of implied volatility based on options trading. Sunrun (RUN) and Omega Therapeutics (OMGA) head into the new week with a high level of short interest outstanding on them. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Power Metals Corp (OTCQB:PWRMF), Cohen Circle Acquisition Corp. I (CCIR), and Hims & Hers Health (HIMS). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Tetra Tech (TTEK) and Owens & Minor (OMI). In Europe, defense stocks Rheinmetall AG (OTCPK:RNMBF) and Thyssenkrupp (OTCPK:TYEKF) are seeing rising short covering amid concerns about European security.
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Domino's Pizza (DPZ), Oneok (OKE), Pure Storage (PSA), and Diamondback Energy (FANG). Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for Chegg (CHGG) and Beyond (BYON) following their reports.
  • All day - The post-IPO quiet period expires on Smithfield Foods (SFD) and Beta Bionics (BBNX) to free up analysts to post ratings.
  • All day - The tender offer for QXO's hostile takeover bid of Beacon Roofing (BECN) will expire.
  • 4:00 p.m. Applied Materials (AMAT) management will participate in a panel discussion during the SPIE 2025 conference.

Tuesday - February 25

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Home Depot (HD), Intuit (INTU), Maplebear (CART), Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP), and Workday (WDAY). Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for Lemonade (LMND) and Lucid Group (LCID) following their reports.
  • All day - Apple (AAPL) will hold its annual meeting. Shareholders will vote on a proposals covering diversity, artificial intelligence, child safety, and charitable topics.
  • All day - Notable investor events include PayPal (PYPL) Investor Day, LiveRamp's (RAMP) Investor Day and Alnylam Pharmaceuticals' (ALNY) R&D Day.
  • 1:00 p.m. Richmond Federal Reserve Bank President Thomas Barkin will speak on "Inflation Then and Now" before the Rotary Club of Richmond.

Wednesday - February 26

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Nvidia (NVDA), Salesforce (CRM), Lowe's (LOW), TJX Companies (TJX), eBay (EBAY), and Snowflake (SNOW). Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for Zevia (ZVIA) and Ambarella (AMBA) after their reports.
  • All day - Notable investor events include 3M Company's (MMM) Investor Day, Littelfuse's (LFUS), Investor Day, Deere & Company's (DE) annual meeting with several shareholder proposals going to a vote, International Money Express' (IMXI) Investor Day, and Diebold Nixdorf's (DBD) Investor Day.
  • All day - Shareholders of Charter (CHTR) and Liberty Broadband (LBRDA) will vote on their planned merger.
  • 10:00 a.m. Amazon (AMZN) will hold a product event. The tech giant is expected to announce a major artificial intelligence upgrade for its voice assistant Alexa. There is also speculation that a new smart home product could be introduced.
  • 12:00 p.m. Atlanta Federal Reserve Bank President Raphael Bostic will participate in conversation on the economic outlook and housing before the Urban Land Institute's annual Housing Opportunity Conference.
  • 5:00 p.m. Nvidia (NVDA) will hold its earnings conference call. Options trading implies a 7% share price swing after the report is released. Notably, over the last two years, Nvidia has seen its two biggest earnings day moves following the February release of the Q4 results. Fabrinet (FN) is the supplier that has had the closest trading correlation to Nvidia over the last three years, moving in tandem with the chip giant 88% of the time.

Thursday - February 27

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Dell Technologies (DELL), HP (HPQ), Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), Toronto-Dominion Bank (TD), and Autodesk (ADSK). Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for Duolingo (DUOL) and Sunrun (RUN) after their reports are released.
  • All day - The two-day Pre-PDAC Mining Showcase will feature keynote speakers and a long list of mining companies presenting.
  • All day - Notable investor events include Frontdoor's (FTDR) Investor Day, Primo Brands' (PRMB) Investor Day, and G. Willi-Food International's (WILC) special meeting to vote on leadership appointments and compensation policy.
  • All day - Perspective Therapeutics (CATX), Varex Imaging (VREX), and Zymeworks (ZYME) are some of the companies participating in the B. Riley Securities Precision Oncology & Radiopharma Conference.
  • All day - The Information's AI Agenda Live event in San Francisco will include participation by Anthropic, PagerDuty (PD), Adobe (ADBE), and IBM (IBM).
  • 2:00 p.m Intel (INTC) will participate in a fireside chat on the company's business and foundry strategy at the Bernstein Technology, Media & Telecom Forum.

Friday - February 28

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Frontline (FRO).
  • All day - Notable investor events include Perrigo Co.'s (PRGO) Investor Day.
  • 8:30 a.m. The core PCE price index report for January will be released. The Fed's favored inflation gauge was up 2.8% year-over-year in both December and November.

Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this story.

data.title: Avalo climbs as Wedbush issues bullish view on lead asset
data.description:
Shares of Avalo Therapeutics (NASDAQ:AVTX) climbed on Friday as Wedbush launched its coverage with an Outperform recommendation and an $18 per share target, citing the potential of the biotech’s lead candidate, AVTX-009.

Analyst Robert Driscoll argued that the antibody therapy designed to target a proinflammatory molecule called IL-1β is currently undergoing a mid-stage trial for the skin disorder hidradenitis suppurativa, which is set to generate data in 2026.

Studies have indicated IL-1β’s role in causing HS, and drugs targeting various IL-1 pathways have shown clinical efficacy in moderate to severe forms of the disease, Driscoll noted.

“We see potential for a best-in-class agent with a derisked approach to treat HS, which has a large and expanding commercial opportunity,” the analyst wrote, arguing that only a third of patients with moderate to severe HS are currently undergoing biologics therapy.

Driscoll added that AVTX could drive further upside as the company targets AVTX-009 for additional indications such as arthritis.

data.title: Point72’s Cohen is ‘pretty negative’ on US economy amid tariffs, immigration, DOGE
data.description:
Steve Cohen, CEO of Point72 Asset Management, said he is “pretty negative” on the U.S. economy due to a likely slowing of growth in the second half of the year, tighter immigration policies, and DOGE.

Speaking at the Future Investment Initiative Institute’s summit in Miami Beach Friday, the founder and chairman of Point72 said that the U.S. economic growth is likely to slow to 1.5% from about 2.5% during the second half of 2025.

“I’m actually pretty negative for the first time in a while,” he said. “It may only last a year or so, but it’s definitely a period where I think the best gains have been had and wouldn’t surprise me to see a significant correction.”

In his bearish view, he cited continued inflation, slowing growth, and the effects U.S.-imposed tariffs and international retaliatory tariffs as drags on the economy.

“Tariffs cannot be positive. It’s a tax,” he said. “And you can imagine tit-for-tat if the U.S. implements a tax on somebody, they’re going to perhaps raise the stakes and raise their tax back.”

Cohen also called Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (or DOGE) “an austerity initiative.”

More on tariffs and the U.S. economy:

  • Study finds Trump tariffs will cost US households $830 per year as President prepares first salvo
  • The Trump Economy 3 Weeks In
  • US to launch probe into impact of Chinese chips, policies on American economy
  • Trump tariffs are ‘largest shock’ to trade in 50 years: Deutsche Bank
  • When Tariffs Were Used As A Weapon, This Was The Price

data.title: Analysts weigh in on Celsius/Alani Nu acquisition
data.description:
Celsius Holdings’ (NASDAQ:CELH) $1.8B acquisition of Alani Lu, one of the fastest growing brands in the energy drink category, “strategically makes sense,” says Needham’s Gerald Pascarelli and will provide accretive growth and scale benefits to the brand.

By doubling down on women energy consumption, Celsius (NASDAQ:CELH) is betting that females will continue to enter the energy drink category at the same rate they have over the past two years, Truist’s Bill Chappell adds, seeing the potential for Celsius (NASDAQ:CELH) to hold or gain share of the female consumer segment versus the more male-oriented brands like Monster (MNST), Red Bull, and C4.

On a combined basis, the company will now have a low-to-mid-teens share of U.S. energy drinks, breaking through what has been an elusive and unsustainable double-digit barrier, Pascarelli says, while at the same time enhance Celsius’ exposure to female consumers, which has in the past been a competitive advantage to CELH.

“We are encouraged by the acquisition of Alani and a better than feared earnings print and expect the shares to be rewarded handsomely as a result,” Pascarelli writes.

Truist’s Chappell, however, sees worrisome signs within the energy drink category that could hamper outsized gains for Celsius/Alani Lu.

“The U.S. energy drink category finished the year with tepid growth and we see no signs of a rebound in the new term,” he says, viewing the entire category as “closer to maturity.” Furthermore, Chappell warns that the M&A activity in the sector along with the heightened competitive efforts from Monster (MNST) and Red Bull “will lead to a hyper promotional landscape in 2025, reducing sales growth and profitability even further.”

The acquisition complements Celsius’ brand positioning and brings access to attractive consumer demographics, but Celsius must first improve its underlying sales/marketing share trends, “with deal-related challenges including integration and sustaining strong sales growth and market share momentum experienced in 2024,” Stifel’s Mark Astrachan write.

Wall Street gives the acquisition a big thumbs up, driving Celsius (CELH) shares up by as much as 35% earlier in the day.

data.title: Stock averages slide 1%, head toward weekly losses, as dour economic data bite
data.description:
Stock losses accelerated Friday afternoon, with investors closing out the week with gloomy economic updates and word of a government investigation into UnitedHealth (UNH) in hand.

The Dow (DJI) tanked more than 600 points, or -1.5%, after Thursday's loss of 1%. The S&P 500 (SP500) -1.4%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -1.8%. 10 of the 11 sectors on the S&P 500 (SP500) fell, led by Consumer Discretionary (XLY) -2.8%. Consumer Staples was the sole advancer.

UnitedHealth (UNH) led decliners on the Dow (DJI). Shares dropped -6.9% after The Wall Street Journal reported the U.S. Department of Justice has launched an investigation into the company’s Medicare billing practices. UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH) declined to comment, and the company later criticized the WSJ over its reporting.

Investors also assessed dour economic reports. “U.S. business activity growth came close to stalling in February as a renewed fall in services output offset faster manufacturing growth,” S&P Global Market Intelligence said. Its February S&P Composite PMI fell to 51.6 from the 52.7 prior print. The services index dropped to 49.7 from 53.0.

“The upbeat mood seen among U.S. businesses at the start of the year has evaporated, replaced with a darkening picture of heightened uncertainty,” Chris Williamson, chief business economist at S&P Global Market Intelligence, said in the report.

Also, the University of Michigan's Consumer Sentiment Index fell to 64.7 in February's final reading, missing the 68.0 consensus, clipped by worries that tariff-induced price increases are approaching.

Separately, January U.S. existing home sales slumped -4.9% to 4.08M versus the 4.13M projection.

The Dow (DJI) for the holiday-shortened week was looking at more than 2% decline. The S&P 500 (SP500) this week notched two record closing highs, but was also facing a weekly loss.

Investors flocked to safety in the bond market, driving the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) down further, by 9 basis points to 4.42%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 9 basis points to 4.20%.

U.S. stocks sold-off Thursday, in part as investors balked at Walmart's (WMT) soft financial outlook.

data.title: This automaker may steer clear of obstacles created by a trade war between the U.S. and China
data.description:
HBSC reiterated a Buy rating on Renault SA (RNSDY) (RNSDY) on its view the European automaker may outperform the sector.

Analyst Pushkar Tendolkar sees new launches driving volume growth and margin support for the French auto giant. Renault's (RNSDY) product mix was highlighted as already having seen strong improvement from the trough in the second half of 2023.

"The new models appear to have been received well by the market and ramp-up of the recent launches along with introduction of 7 new models (including the Bigster, an important earnings driver) in 2025, holds the key for 3-4% sales growth and margin support in a difficult 2025 for the industry."

Renault (RNSDY) was also given credit for managing an elevated inventory situation quite well in the back half of 2023, which gives HSBC confidence it can handle the rush of new models.

HSBC said Renault (RNSDY) is the preferred play in the European auto sector. Importantly, the company has no direct China or U.S. exposure and is seen having pricing potential that is underestimated by the market. On the demand side, enhanced content in vehicles is expected to be well received by consumers.

As a kicker, Renault's (RNSDY) balance sheet was called strong and has potential for further inflow through the exit of the Nissan (OTCPK:NSANY) stake.
